title: Why Evolution & Animals Matter
body:
Evolution matters because it is central to medicine, agriculture, conservation, and our understanding of ourselves. Antibiotic resistance is evolution in real time. Viruses evolve too, which is why tracking mutations is essential during outbreaks and pandemics.

Studying animals matters because it challenges human exceptionalism in useful ways. As we learn more about animal cognition and emotion, the ethical case for better treatment becomes harder to ignore. That affects farming, habitat protection, and scientific research.

The subject also matters because it explains biodiversity. Without evolution, the patterns in the living world look accidental. With evolution, they become connected and intelligible.
keyPoints: Evolution is essential to medicine and public health
Animal cognition raises serious ethical questions
Conservation depends on understanding adaptation and extinction
Agriculture is shaped by selective breeding and co-evolution
The topic helps explain human instincts and behaviour
